steps.title: Solve the system by elimination
steps.content: Subtract the second equation from the first: $$(3x+6)-(3x+4)=4y-2y,$$ giving $2=2y$.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: The correct answer is $1$. Subtracting the second equation from the first equation in the given system of equations yields $\left(3x-3x\right)+\left(6-4\right)=4y-2y$, which is equivalent to$0+2=2y$, or $2=2y$. Dividing each side of this equation by $2$ yields $1=y$.
